name: Siam (1817–1855)

colors.symbolism: the chakra and the white elephant within it

description: A red field bearing a white elephant set inside a white chakra (Chakri wheel) at the centre.
history: Under Rama II a white elephant — the auspicious royal beast of Siam — was added inside the chakra in 1817. This design was used until 1855, when Rama IV replaced it with the elephant alone.
